plugin_desc: Red Lion / N-Tron industrial Ethernet switch-specific SNMP collection (NTRON714FX6-MIB, IANA PEN 28381). In addition to standard IF-MIB interface metrics, it collects the N-Tron private dual power-input status object (ntronPowerFault). The raw codes (1=faultNone meaning both inputs OK, 2=faultPower1 meaning input 1 lost, 3=faultPower2 meaning input 2 lost) are normalized via telegraf processors.enum to 1=healthy / 2=fault. The N-Tron MIB exposes only RAM/Flash totals (no used or free, so memory utilization cannot be computed), no CPU object, no pollable numeric temperature (the onboard sensor is exposed only via the web UI / N-View, not SNMP) and no fan object (N-Tron switches are fanless and convection-cooled), so CPU, memory, temperature and fan are not collected (N/A). The power-fault object is defined in the MIB but is firmware-dependent; on firmware that does not return it the plugin degrades to a clean IF-MIB baseline. Supported brands/models — Red Lion / N-Tron industrial Ethernet switches (714FX6 and N-Tron series, IANA PEN 28381).
name: Switch

metrics.name: device_psu_state
metrics.description: This metric reports the power-supply health of the Red Lion / N-Tron device, derived from the private dual power-input status object (ntronPowerFault). The raw codes (1=faultNone meaning both inputs OK, 2=faultPower1 meaning input 1 lost, 3=faultPower2 meaning input 2 lost) are normalized via telegraf processors.enum so that 1 = healthy and 2 = fault. A value greater than 1 indicates a lost power input that warrants investigation.

metrics.name: interface_ifHCInOctets
metrics.description: This metric indicates the average rate of bytes received over the past 5 minutes, measured in bytes per second, from the 64-bit IF-MIB ifHCInOctets counter (ifXTable). The 64-bit counter avoids the rapid wrap of the 32-bit ifInOctets on high-speed switch ports, keeping the rate accurate.

metrics.name: interface_ifHCOutOctets
metrics.description: This metric calculates the average rate of bytes sent over the past 5 minutes, measured in bytes per second, from the 64-bit IF-MIB ifHCOutOctets counter (ifXTable). The 64-bit counter avoids the rapid wrap of the 32-bit ifOutOctets on high-speed switch ports, keeping the rate accurate.
